name: S4 flavor symmetry
summary: Non-abelian discrete flavor symmetry based on S4 (permutation group of 4 objects). C.S. Lam proved that S4 is the minimal finite group whose representation theory naturally produces the tribimaximal lepton-mixing pattern (theta_12 ~ 35deg, theta_13 = 0, theta_23 = 45deg) as a leading-order prediction. Deviations from tribimaximal mixing (now measured: theta_13 ~ 8.6deg) require S4-breaking corrections.

addresses_puzzles.note: S4 alone gives tribimaximal mixing in the lepton sector; deviations to accommodate theta_13 > 0 require either symmetry-breaking corrections or extensions to S4 x Z_n. Quark-sector predictions are weaker.
S4 representations naturally split fermions into triplets and singlets, providing texture zeros and partial-hierarchy explanations in specific models.

touches_benchmarks.note: Leading-order tribimaximal predicts theta_13 = 0; observed theta_13 ~ 8.6deg requires S4-breaking. Modern modular-S4 variants can fit theta_13 naturally.
S4 sum rules constrain the Majorana phases entering m_betabeta; specific models give sharp predictions testable at KamLAND-Zen and LEGEND.

introduces.note: Flavon VEV pattern, alignment parameters, mixing with charged leptons.

history: Brown, Deshpande, Mahanthappa, Senjanovic (1984) early use of S4 in flavor physics. Lam (2008), 'Determining horizontal symmetry from neutrino mixing', Phys. Rev. Lett. 101, 121602, arXiv:0804.2622, proved S4 is the unique minimal group for tribimaximal. Modular-S4 variants (Penedo-Petcov 2018, arXiv:1806.11040) revive the framework after theta_13 measurement.

references.citation: Lam, 'Determining horizontal symmetry from neutrino mixing', Phys. Rev. Lett. 101, 121602 (2008)
Penedo and Petcov, 'Lepton masses and mixing from modular S4 symmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 939, 292 (2019)
